---
title: "4.2.2 Assembly languages"
subtitle: "Software P167-P168"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)

## Assembly Languages

### Why some programmers use assembly

Fewer programmers write programs in an <span class="term">assembly language</span> (汇编语言).

Those who do may use assembly language:

::: {.tight-list}
- to make use of special hardware
- to make use of special machine-dependent instructions
- to write code that does not take up much space in primary memory
- to write code that performs a task very quickly
:::

## Assembly Language Example

### Adding two numbers

The textbook gives this typical assembly language example:

```text
LDA    First
ADD    Second
STO    Sum
```

It consists of three statements.

## Mnemonics

### What the statements mean

To understand the program, the programmer needs to know the meaning of the mnemonics:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">LDA</span> means load value of the variable into the accumulator
- <span class="term">ADD</span> means add value of the variable to the value stored in the accumulator
- <span class="term">STO</span> means replace the value of the variable by the value stored in the accumulator
:::

## Assembler

### Translation into machine code

Assembly language needs to be translated into <span class="term">machine code</span> using an <span class="term">assembler</span> in order to run.

This means:

::: {.tight-list}
- the program is written in assembly language
- the assembler translates it into machine code
- the machine code can then be used by the computer
:::

## Classroom Check

### Explain assembly language

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- assembly language is a <span class="term">low-level language</span>
- it is dependent on computer hardware
- it can use special hardware and machine-dependent instructions
- mnemonics such as LDA, ADD and STO represent operations
- an <span class="term">assembler</span> is needed to translate assembly language into machine code
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Translators

The next deck compares compilers, interpreters and assemblers.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 4 Directory / 返回 Unit 4 目录](../../index.html)
